methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $2.3B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $500M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
Divergent, a Series E robotics and manufacturing platform founded by Lukas and Kevin Czinger, carries a current post-money valuation of $2.3B following $600M+ in total capital raised. The valuation assessment draws from a comparables universe of 8 private-stage peers in robotics and manufacturing—including 1X Technologies, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity—alongside 1 public comparable, with peer valuations ranging from $500M to $10.0B.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a 1.4x step-up multiplier from Divergent's $2.3B Series E post-money baseline, implies a valuation of $3.2B. Peer-median analysis across the 8-company comparables set yields a median valuation of $1.7B, reflecting broad dispersion within the robotics sector. The peer range spans $500M to $10.0B, indicating substantial heterogeneity by maturity and capital intensity.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.4B (low) to $3.7B (high), with a median of $2.5B. The current $2.3B valuation sits slightly below the triangulated median, aligned with peer-median guidance but below stage-progression expectations. Primary uncertainties include the wide peer-set dispersion and the limited applicability of public comparables given the early-stage, capital-intensive nature of the robotics sector. Stage progression and peer median divergence—spanning a 1.9x spread—reflects inherent ambiguity in forward-valuation multiples for this asset class.
